13 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • U.S. Forces Complete Mission in Syria to Transfer ISIS Detainees to Iraq CENTCOM 13 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) completed a transfer mission following a nighttime flight from northeastern Syria to Iraq on Feb. 12 to help ensure ISIS detainees remain secure in detention facilities.

  • Lethal Kinetic Strike, Feb. 13, 2026 SOUTHCOM 13 Feb 2026 -- On Feb. 13, at the direction of the commander of U.S. Southern Command, Gen. Francis L. Donovan, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations.
  • U.S. Navy destroyer, supply ship collide in Caribbean Sea People's Daily 13 Feb 2026 -- A U.S. Navy destroyer and a supply ship collided in the Caribbean, injuring two U.S. Navy sailors, Fox News reported on Thursday, citing a U.S. Southern Command spokesperson.

  • Actions to Implement President Trump's Vision for Venezuelan Oil US Dept. of State 13 Feb 2026 -- The Trump Administration is rapidly implementing President Trump's vision to reopen and develop Venezuela's oil industry for the shared benefit of the American and Venezuelan people.
  • Delivering Priority Medical Supplies for the Venezuelan People US Dept. of State 13 Feb 2026 -- At President Trump's direction, today the United States airlifted an initial 25 pallets—over 6,000 kilograms—of priority medical supplies to the Venezuelan people as part of the administration's three-phase plan for Venezuela's stabilization, recovery, and transition.

  • Coast Guard offloads over $133.5 million in illicit drugs interdicted in Eastern Pacific Ocean US Coast Guard 13 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Seneca's crew offloaded approximately 17,750 pounds of cocaine worth more than $133.5 million in Port Everglades, Friday.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Rubio Says World At 'Defining Moment,' Jets Into Munich RFE/RL 13 Feb 2026 -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the world is at a "defining moment" as he headed for the Munich Security Conference (MSC), where he was scheduled to arrive at 08:00 a.m. local time on February 13.

  • Department of War Partners With Department of Energy in Historic Nuclear Energy Initiative DoD 13 Feb 2026 -- The Department of War (DoW) and the Department of Energy (DoE) are partnering to mark a historic milestone in advancing America's nuclear energy landscape and strengthening national security.
  • Department of War Releases Letter to Defense Industrial Base and Acquisition Stakeholders Seeking Revolutionary FAR Overhaul Phase 2 Input DoD 13 Feb 2026 -- On February 10, 2026, the Department of War released a letter to the defense industrial base and acquisition stakeholders announcing issuance of Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Phase 1 class deviations and requesting substantive input to help shape Phase 2 regulatory reforms.
  • DOW Aims to Accelerate Advanced Materials Processing DoD 13 Feb 2026 -- The Department of War (DOW) announced today the September 17, 2025 investment on a five-year contract totaling nearly $9.2 million to the national advanced materials Manufacturing Innovation Institute, LIFT, to address manufacturing challenges associated with ceramic matrix composites (CMC).
  • Department Seeks Counsel of Industry Leaders to Advance Arsenal of Freedom DoD News 13 Feb 2026 -- The War Department is asking industry experts to help the department better work with the defense industrial base, eliminate bureaucratic bloat in the acquisition process and more quickly field weapons to warfighters.
  • Coast Guard commissions USCGC Frederick Mann (WPC 1160) in Kodiak, Alaska US Coast Guard 13 Feb 2026 -- The Coast Guard commissioned its newest and sixth Arctic District Fast Response Cutter (FRC), Coast Guard Cutter Frederick Mann (WPC 1160), for official entry into its service fleet during a ceremony held in Kodiak, Friday.
  • CNO Remarks to Navy Submarine League Pacific Southwest - As Prepared US Navy 13 Feb 2026
  • CNO Keynote Remarks at AFCEA WEST - As Prepared US Navy 13 Feb 2026
  • CNO Presents U.S. Navy Fighting Instructions - As Prepared US Navy 13 Feb 2026
  • Keel Laid for Future USNS Ruth Bader Ginsburg US Navy Press Release 13 Feb 2026 -- The keel for the future USNS Ruth Bader Ginsburg (T-AO 212) was authenticated at the General Dynamics NASSCO shipyard on Feb. 13. The event marked a major construction milestone for the John Lewis-class replenishment oiler.

  • NATO announces Arctic Sentry - enhancing NATO's presence in the Arctic and the High North NATO Allied Air Command 13 Feb 2026 -- Allied Command Operations (ACO) announces the multi-domain activity - Arctic Sentry - which will further strengthen NATO's posture in the Arctic and High North as persistent NATO presence in the region grows.
  • Doorstep statement by the NATO Secretary General at the Munich Security Conference NATO 13 Feb 2026

  • Russia's Next-Door 'EU Neighbors' Secretly Plotting to Obtain Nukes - Reports Sputnik 13 Feb 2026 -- European countries bordering Russia have secretly begun discussing the possibility of developing their own "nuclear deterrent" capabilities for the first time since the Cold War, Bloomberg reported Friday, citing sources familiar with the talks between certain governments and militaries.

  • Russia Sets Task of Building Up Combat Potential Under CSTO Chairmanship Sputnik 13 Feb 2026 -- Russia is setting a task of building up the combat potential and weapons, taking into account current challenges and conflicts, within the framework of its chairmanship in the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO), Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Alexander Pankin said on Friday.
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with members of parliament and ambassadors of the CSTO member states Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 13 Feb 2026

  • In Munich, the President Met with the Leadership and Employees of the First Ukrainian-German Joint Drone Production Venture President of Ukraine 13 Feb 2026
  • Support of USD 38 billion for Ukraine for drones, air defence and Patriot missiles: outcomes of one of the most successful Ramstein-format meetings Ukraine Government 13 Feb 2026 -- As a result of the Ukraine Defense Contact Group meeting (Ramstein format), partner countries confirmed one of the largest support budgets for Ukraine, totalling $38 billion for 2026.
  • Norway and Allies Contribute to New Military Support Package for Ukraine Government of Norway 13 Feb 2026 -- Norway, with several Allies, will finance a new support package of defence materiel for Ukraine amounting to approximately NOK 5 billion.
  • Sweden strengthens Ukraine's defence with a contribution of USD 100 million to US package Government Offices of Sweden 13 Feb 2026 -- Sweden is contributing USD 100 million to the US support package Prioritised Ukraine Requirements List (PURL).
  • A. Sprūds in Brussels: Ukraine's involvement in the European Defence Fund will strengthen security across Europe Latvia MOD 13 Feb 2026 -- On Wednesday, February 11, Minister of Defence Andris Sprūds participated in the European Union Foreign Affairs Council meeting of defence ministers in Brussels.
  • Minister of National Defence took part in the Ukraine Defence Contact Group meeting in Brussels Lithuania MND 13 Feb 2026 -- Minister of National Defence Robertas Kaunas attended the 33rd meeting in the NATO Defence Contact Group held together with the NATO Defence Ministerial in Brussels.
  • Innovation for the front line, air defence missiles and the development of drone assault units: new support for Ukraine following the 33rd Ramstein-format meeting Ukraine MOD 13 Feb 2026
  • MoD launches testing of updated Reserve+ deferment for parents with three or more children Ukraine MOD 13 Feb 2026
  • International Legions: Ukraine values every foreign volunteer fighter Ukraine MOD 13 Feb 2026
  • $38 billion in support of Ukraine for drones, air defence and Patriot missiles: outcomes of one of the most successful Ramstein-format meetings Ukraine MOD 13 Feb 2026

  • U.S. lawmakers urge opposition to approve special defense budget CNA 13 Feb 2026 -- A group of U.S. lawmakers from both chambers of Congress on Thursday wrote to Legislative Speaker Han Kuo-yu (韓國瑜) and several political party leaders, calling on them to support the funding for Taiwan's special defense budget.
  • Chief of the General Staff Admiral Mei Chia-shu Inspects Wuqiu Defense Area - Commends Personnel for Their Hard Work in Defending the Frontline ROC MND 13 Feb 2026

  • HMAS Toowoomba transits South China Sea Australia DOD 13 Feb 2026 -- Royal Australian Navy Anzac-class long-range frigate HMAS Toowoomba has safely transited the South China Sea in the vicinity of the Spratly Islands as part of her 2026 regional presence deployment.

  • QA-Industry Conclave showcases digital transformation as the cornerstone of trusted & time-bound defence quality assurance India PIB 13 Feb 2026 -- The Quality Assurance (QA)-Industry Conclave themed 'Traceability, Speed and Trust - Leveraging Technology for Smarter Quality Assurance' took place in New Delhi on February 13, 2026
  • DAP 2026 proposes transformative leap in India's defence procurement framework India PIB 13 Feb 2026 -- The Defence Acquisition Procedure (DAP) 2026, the draft of which was released recently by the Ministry of Defence to replace the existing 2020 version, has been designed to bolster self-reliance, accelerate acquisition timelines, and empower the Indian defence industrial ecosystem
  • THE INDIAN AIR FORCE (IAF) CONDUCTS IN-SITU JOINT EXERCISE WITH ROYAL THAI AIR FORCE (RTAF) IN INDIAN OCEAN REGION India PIB 13 Feb 2026 -- The Indian Air Force (IAF) conducted an In-situ Exercise with the Royal Thai Air Force (RTAF) in the Indian Ocean Region from 09 to 12 February 2026, strengthening military cooperation and enhancing mutual understanding between the two countries

  • Shamkhani: Iran's missile power is 'non-negotiable' IRNA 13 Feb 2026 -- Ali Shamkhani, Representative of the Leader in the Supreme Defense Council, has reiterated that Iran's missile power is a non-negotiable red line and that Tehran will respond decisively and appropriately to any aggression.
  • Shamkhani warns of 'strong, decisive' response to any adventurism against Iran Press TV 13 Feb 2026 -- Iran will deliver a "strong, decisive, and appropriate" response to any adventurism against it, Rear Admiral Ali Shamkhani warned, underscoring Tehran's high military readiness amid increasing US threats.

  • Munich Security Conference: Speech and Dialogue (Summary) Ministry of Defense of Japan 13 Feb 2026
  • Japan-Canada Defense Ministerial Meeting (Summary) Ministry of Defense of Japan 13 Feb 2026
  • Japan-Italy Defense Ministerial Meeting (Summary) Ministry of Defense of Japan 13 Feb 2026
  • Japan-New Zealand Defense Ministerial Meeting Ministry of Defense of Japan 13 Feb 2026
  • Meeting between Defense Minister Koizumi and NATO Secretary General Rutte Ministry of Defense of Japan 13 Feb 2026
  • Japan-UK Defense Ministerial Meeting (Summary) Ministry of Defense of Japan 13 Feb 2026
  • Meeting with Members of the U.S. Senate Ministry of Defense of Japan 13 Feb 2026

  • Air Chief Marshal Zaheer Ahmed Baber Sidhu, NI (M), HJ, Chief of Air Staff, Pakistan Air Force, during his official visit to Indonesia, called on President Prabowo Subianto. ISPR Pakistan 13 Feb 2026
  • The newly commissioned, state-of-the-art Pakistan Navy Ship KHAIBAR, during its maiden voyage from Türkiye to Pakistan, visited Aksaz Naval Base, Türkiye, and participated in Exercise TURGUTREIS-XIII and joint patrols with ships of the Turkish Navy. ISPR Pakistan 13 Feb 2026

  • UK spends over £400 million on long-range weapons as cooperation scales up with European allies UK MOD 13 Feb 2026 -- UK will spend over £400 million this financial year on hypersonic and long-range weapons including joint projects with France, Germany, and Italy.
  • Hypersonic missiles development accelerated by new contract UK MOD 13 Feb 2026 -- The development of the new hypersonic missiles will speed up thanks to a £12 million contract which will benefit British-based businesses.

  • Survey: strengthening NATO collective defence and creating opportunities for Estonian businesses in foreign markets seen as the top foreign policy priorities Estonia MFA 13 Feb 2026 -- Today, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs published its second public opinion survey on foreign policy issues, conducted at the end of last year.
  • Pevkur at NATO Defence Ministers' Meeting: Allies must show rapid progress on defence spending and capabilities ahead of Ankara Estonia MoD 13 Feb 2026 -- The NATO Defence Ministers' meeting in Brussels focused on increasing defence spending and ensuring that European Allies and Canada take greater responsibility for the defence of Europe.

  • Minister Jaromír Zůna attended key meetings in NATO and the EU, discussed future threats Czech Republic Ministry of Defence & Armed Forces 13 Feb 2026 -- Defence Minister Jaromír Zůna went for his first visit in Brussels after assuming office to attend the NATO Defence Ministerial meeting, the European Foreign Affairs Council - Defence and the Ukraine Defence Contact Group (UDCG).

  • Minister Aukrust's address at the Norad Conference Peace Out Government of Norway 13 Feb 2026 -- The Minister of International Development Åsmund Aukrust's address at the Norad Conference Peace Out, 28 January, Sentrum Scene.

  • Statement by President von der Leyen at the Transatlantic Forum with Bavarian Minister-President Söder, NATO Secretary-General Rutte and German Foreign Minister Wadephul European Commission 13 Feb 2026

  • Latvia and allies outline security priorities at NATO defence ministers meeting Latvia MOD 13 Feb 2026 -- On Thursday, February 12, Minister of Defence Andris Sprūds participated in the NATO Defence ministers' meeting in Brussels, where allies agreed on further steps to strengthen deterrence and defence capabilities in preparation for the upcoming Alliance summit in Ankara this July.
  • Andris Sprūds: Latvia will take part in the NATO Arctic Sentry operation Latvia MOD 13 Feb 2026 -- On Wednesday, 11 February, NATO launched Operation Arctic Sentry. The operation is aimed at strengthening NATO's position in the Arctic and the High North, while expanding the Alliance's sustained presence in the region.

  • Defence Minister's meeting with representatives of the European defence industry, in Munich Romania - Ministry of National Defence 13 Feb 2026 -- On Friday, February 13, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a series of meetings with high-level representatives of the European defence industry, the technology sector and strategic analysis organizations, as part of the Munich Security Conference (MSC).
  • Romania and Luxembourg - committed to enhance cooperation in the field of European defence and security Romania - Ministry of National Defence 13 Feb 2026 -- On Friday, February 13th, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a meeting with the Minister of Defence of the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg, Yuriko Backes, as part of the Munich Security Conference.
  • Minister of National Defence, Head of Romanian delegation at the Münich Security Conference Romania - Ministry of National Defence 13 Feb 2026 -- Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, will participate, between February 13th-15th, in the Munich Security Conference (MSC), together with the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana-Silvia Țoiu, and the General Director of the Romanian National Cyber Security Directorate, Dan Cîmpean.
  • High-level meetings and consolidated commitments to defence, in Brussels Romania - Ministry of National Defence 13 Feb 2026 -- On Thursday, February 12th, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, participated in the Meeting of NATO Ministers of Defence, held at the Alliance Headquarters, in Brussels.

  • Army eyes air defense capability Philippine News Agency 13 Feb 2026 -- The Philippine Army (PA) is looking to develop an air defense capability that would allow the service to support the Comprehensive Archipelagic Defense Concept (CADC) now being operationalized by the country's military forces.
  • Most advanced PH ships participating in 'Balikatan' Philippine News Agency 13 Feb 2026 -- The most advanced and up-to-date Philippine Navy (PN) naval vessels will be deployed to this year's "Balikatan" exercises with the United States military.

  • Disclosure Israeli Retired Heads in the General Security Services 13 Feb 2026 -- "By his actions and behavior, the Prime Minister is harming the entire security establishment, and in particular the General Security Service, its managers and employees, who work day and night for Israel's security. This is despicable and vile conduct on the part of the person primarily responsible for the policy that led Israel to one of the most serious failures in its history."

  • The Chief of the General Staff in Gaza Strip Israel Defense Forces 13 Feb 2026
  • Weekly Counterterrorism Summary in the Central Command Israel Defense Forces 13 Feb 2026

  • Israeli settler assaults in West Bank 'constitute criminal aggression': Hamas Press TV 13 Feb 2026 -- Hamas has condemned latest assaults by Israeli settlers and the relentless army offensive across the occupied West Bank, as "systematic terrorist practices" run by the occupying regime.

  • Gaza: Lifesaving aid operations continue despite restrictions UN News 13 Feb 2026 -- Humanitarians in the Gaza Strip continue to face impediments in their efforts to deliver lifesaving aid to the population.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Haiti UNOCHA 13 Feb 2026

  • Diplomacy, 2016 SCS award 'cores' of PH maritime statecraft - DFA Philippine News Agency 13 Feb 2026 -- Diplomacy remains central to the Philippines' maritime statecraft, the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) said Friday, underscoring the need for approaches that "lead to positive outcomes" in managing tensions in the West Philippine Sea.

  • UNHCR seeks support for solutions as 5.4 million Afghans return since late 2023 UNHCR 13 Feb 2026 -- This is a summary of what was said by Arafat Jamal, UNHCR Representative to Afghanistan - to whom quoted text may be attributed - at today's press briefing at the Palais des Nations in Geneva.

  • Syria transition gains ground with Kurdish deal, but violence and humanitarian strain persist UN News 13 Feb 2026 -- Syria's fragile political transition has gained fresh momentum with a landmark agreement between Damascus and Kurdish authorities in the northeast, but renewed violence in the south, Israeli incursions and deep humanitarian needs underscore how precarious the path to stability remains, senior UN officials told the Security Council on Friday.
  • At the Security Council, OCHA calls for concerted efforts to prevent violence in Syria UNOCHA 13 Feb 2026 -- Briefing to the Security Council on the humanitarian situation in Syria by Lisa Doughten, Director, Financing and Partnerships Division, OCHA, on behalf of Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator
  • Security Council Speakers Welcome Ceasefire Agreement in Syria, Call for Inclusive Political Transition with Full Participation for Women, Kurdish Minority UN Security Council 13 Feb 2026 -- Welcoming a comprehensive agreement reached between the Syrian Government and the Syrian Democratic Forces two weeks ago, UN experts and Council members encouraged that country to stay on the path of inclusive political transition, with full participation for women and the Kurdish minority.
  • The UK welcomes the agreement reached between the Syrian Government and the Syrian Democratic Forces to bring an end to the fighting in North-East Syria: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 13 Feb 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Syria.

  • Sudan: RSF violations in capture of El Fasher amount to war crimes OHCHR 13 Feb 2026 -- The Rapid Support Forces unleashed "a wave of intense violence ... shocking in its scale and brutality" during its final offensive to capture the besieged city of El Fasher last October, committing widespread atrocities that amount to war crimes and possible crimes against humanity, says a report published Friday by the UN Human Rights Office.
  • Sudan Statement WFP 13 Feb 2026 -- WFP is outraged by recent attacks on its trucks, assets and facilities in Sudan. Four incidents over the past 10 days have resulted in humanitarians killed or injured while delivering life-saving food assistance to the most vulnerable civilians.
  • 'Like a scene out of a horror movie': UN report warns of war crimes in Sudan's El Fasher UN News 13 Feb 2026 -- Paramilitary forces in Sudan unleashed "a wave of intense violence...shocking in its scale and brutality" during their final offensive to capture the besieged city of El Fasher last October, committing atrocities that amount to war crimes and possible crimes against humanity, according to a report released on Friday by the UN human rights office, OHCHR.

  • Violence no child can outrun - South Sudan's escalating crisis UNICEF 13 Feb 2026 -- This is a summary of what was said by UNICEF Representative (a.i) to South Sudan, Obia Achieng, to whom quoted text may be attributed - at today's press briefing at the Palais des Nations in Geneva.
  • Security Council Press Statement on South Sudan UN Security Council 13 Feb 2026

  • ICC rejects Duterte appeal on fitness to stand trial Philippine News Agency 13 Feb 2026 -- The International Criminal Court (ICC) has rejected the appeal of former president Rodrigo Duterte's camp to overturn the earlier ruling that deemed him fit to stand trial.

  • READOUT: Ranking Member Shaheen, Senators Wicker, Murphy, Kelly, Gallego and Congressman Himes Meeting with Bosnian Presidency Member Denis Becirovic and High Representative Christian Schmidt US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 13 Feb 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and Roger Wicker (R-MS), Chair of the Senate Armed Services Committee, along with Senators Chris Murphy (D-CT), Mark Kelly (D-AZ), Ruben Gallego (D-AZ) and Congressman Jim Himes (D-CT), met with Denis Becirovic, member of the Presidency of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Christian Schmidt, High Representative for Bosnia and Herzegovina, on the sidelines of the 2026 Munich Security Conference.

  • African Union Calls on Israel to Withdraw Its Recognition of Somaliland Sputnik 13 Feb 2026 -- The African Union called on Israel to withdraw its recognition of the self-proclaimed Republic of Somaliland, according to a communique published following a meeting of the AU Peace and Security Council.

News Reports

  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Senators Coons, Murphy, Kaine, Booker, Van Hollen, Duckworth and Rosen Publish Major New Report on True Cost of Trump Administration's Third Country Deportation Deals US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 13 Feb 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Chris Coons (D-DE), Chris Murphy (D-CT), Tim Kaine (D-VA), Chris Van Hollen (D-MD), Cory Booker (D-NJ), Tammy Duckworth (D-IL) and Jacky Rosen (D-NV) released a comprehensive report detailing how the Trump Administration's secret deportation deals are undermining U.S. interests and coming at great cost to taxpayers.
  • Minnesota officials "cautiously optimistic" about end of federal immigration crackdown, demand investigation People's Daily 13 Feb 2026 -- Minnesota officials on Thursday responded with cautious optimism to Trump administration's announcement that it would end large-scale federal immigration enforcement operations in the state and demanded investigation.
  • U.S. judge blocks Trump from stripping South Sudanese deportation protections People's Daily 13 Feb 2026 -- A U.S. federal judge on Thursday blocked President Donald Trump's administration from ending temporary protections from deportation for South Sudanese nationals in the United States.

  • Sweden and Ukraine agree to cooperate on cybersecurity Government Offices of Sweden 13 Feb 2026 -- Cyber attacks are one of the tactics used by Russia in its war against Ukraine. Sweden and Ukraine have now reached an agreement aimed at strengthening Ukraine's resilience to cyber attacks, which also enhances Sweden's capability to cope with sophisticated cyber threats.

  • Twenty-Seven Members and Associates of Tren de Aragua Splinter Faction Anti-Tren Charged With 2024 Double Murder in New York and Sex Trafficking, Kidnapping, Racketeering, and Other Offenses US Dept. of Justice 13 Feb 2026 -- A 38-count superseding indictment (S4 indictment) was unsealed today charging 27 members of the Anti-Tren faction of the designated foreign terrorist organization Tren de Aragua (TDA or TdA) with offenses including racketeering conspiracy, murder in aid of racketeering in connection with an April 15, 2024, double murder in the Bronx, New York, murder-for-hire conspiracy, kidnapping in aid of racketeering, sex trafficking and sex trafficking conspiracy, and other offenses.

  • China warns of 'measures' following CIA's Chinese-language recruitment drive Press TV 13 Feb 2026 -- Beijing has warned it would take "all necessary measures" against foreign spies, hitting back after the US spy agency posted a recruitment video aimed at Chinese military personnel.

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Burkina Faso Foreign Minister Karamoko Traore Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 13 Feb 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on February 13, 2026 PRC MofA 13 Feb 2026
  • Wang Yi Meets with Argentine Foreign Minister Pablo Quirno PRC MofA 13 Feb 2026 -- Joining Hands to Set the Ship of History on the Right Course
  • Wang Yi Meets with Ukrainian Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha PRC MofA 13 Feb 2026 -- On February 13, 2026 local time, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with Ukrainian Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference.
  • China recovers first rocket booster at sea, marks milestone in reusable technology Global Times 13 Feb 2026 -- China completed its first maritime recovery of a rocket booster on Friday, as search and recovery teams retrieved the first-stage of a Long March-10 carrier rocket from the sea, a milestone in advancement of reusable launch vehicles technologies, Central China Television reported on Friday, citing the China Manned Space Agency.

  • Taiwan signs trade deal with U.S. to cut tariffs, open markets (update) CNA 13 Feb 2026 -- Taiwan and the United States signed a reciprocal trade agreement in Washington on Thursday, capping U.S. tariffs on Taiwanese goods at 15 percent and granting preferential market access for U.S. industrial and agricultural exports, including automobiles, beef, and pork.
  • Opposition parties vow strict review of Taiwan-U.S. trade deal CNA 13 Feb 2026 -- Taiwan's opposition parties on Friday vowed to rigorously scrutinize the newly signed Taiwan-U.S. trade agreement, with the Kuomintang (KMT) expressing "grave concerns" about its potential negative impacts.
  • Trade deal with U.S. secures best tariff rates, safeguards local sectors: Cabinet CNA 13 Feb 2026 -- Taiwan has secured highly favorable tariff treatment while protecting key domestic industries under its newly signed reciprocal trade agreement with the United States, the Cabinet said Thursday, at the conclusion of a 10‑month negotiation process on the deal.
  • Any attempt to split the motherland and oppose reunification is sheer fantasy: Mainland spokesperson on Lai's interview with AFP Global Times 13 Feb 2026 -- There is only one China in the world, and Taiwan is part of China. This cannot be changed by anyone's empty rhetoric or political manipulations, said Chen Binhua, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, on Friday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 13 Feb 2026

  • North Korea warns of 'terrible response' to drone incursions from South Press TV 13 Feb 2026 -- The Democratic People's Republic of Korea has warned of a "terrible response" in the event of another drone incursion from South Korea.

  • In Munich, UN Nuclear Chief Says New Inspection Regime With Iran 'Technically Possible' RFE/RL 13 Feb 2026 -- Developing a inspection regime to underpin any new Iran nuclear arrangement is "absolutely possible," though extremely difficult, the head of the United Nations atomic watchdog said.
  • Eslami: Enemies pursuing 'pressure from outside, collapse from inside' strategy against Iran IRNA 13 Feb 2026 -- Mohammad Eslami, Vice President and Head of the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (AEOI), says enemies of Iran are pursuing a strategy of "pressure from outside and collapse from inside" against Iran.
  • Iran's progress 'miraculous, extraordinary' despite sanctions: Nuclear chief Press TV 13 Feb 2026 -- Head of the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (AEOI) Mohammad Eslami says the country has succeeded in making remarkable progress in the nuclear industry despite the enemies' all-out pressures.
  • Chinese Tech Offers Blueprint For Iran's Digital Crackdown, New Report Says RFE/RL 13 Feb 2026 -- A new report by a British human rights group says Iran's system of Internet control draws extensively on Chinese technology, enabling authorities to disconnect the country's 93 million people from the global Internet during the height of January's anti-government protests.
  • Netanyahu voices skepticism about potential U.S.-Iran deal, calls for missile curbs People's Daily 13 Feb 2026 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that he had "expressed general scepticism about the nature of any deal with Iran" and if a deal between the United States and Iran is indeed reached, it must include "the ballistic missiles and the Iranian proxies," demands Tehran has previously rejected.

  • Trump calls Herzog 'disgraceful' for refusing to pardon Netanyahu Press TV 13 Feb 2026 -- US President Donald Trump called Israeli president Isaac Herzog "disgraceful" for refusing to pardon prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu, escalating his public pressure campaign as Netanyahu's corruption trial drags on.

  • G7 Rapid Response Mechanism Announcement on Polish Accession Canada 13 Feb 2026
  • Poland Joins the G7 Rapid Response Mechanism Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 13 Feb 2026 -- Poland has joined the G7 Rapid Response Mechanism (G7 RRM) as a new associate member.

  • The Daily Press Briefing (02/13/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 13 Feb 2026

  • Prime Minister to warn that Europe must move away from overdependence on the US, to interdependence - and a more European NATO Gov.UK 13 Feb 2026 -- Europe must shift from overdependence on the United States and towards a more European NATO, underpinned by deeper links between the UK and the EU, the Prime Minister will say today.
  • PM meeting with Chancellor Merz of Germany and President Macron of France: 13 February 2026 Gov.UK 13 Feb 2026

  • "We are asserting our freedom with our neighbours" Germany - Federal Government 13 Feb 2026 -- In a world of increasing tensions, Europe must preserve its own values, protect its freedom and build on its strengths, emphasised Federal Chancellor Friedrich Merz in his speech at the Munich Security Conference (MSC). Europe, he said, is more valuable today than ever before.
  • Munich Security Conference 2026: Germany as host in times of geopolitical upheaval Germany Federal Foreign Office 13 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul is attending the 62nd Munich Security Conference from 13 to 15 February 2026. Read on to find out what this year's conference title "Under Destruction" is all about and which issues will be at the forefront.
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with German Chancellor Merz US Dept. of State 13 Feb 2026

  • Minister Anand meets with France's Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs Canada 13 Feb 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, today met with Jean-Noël Barrot, France's Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs, at the Munich Security Conference, held from February 13 to 15, 2026, in Germany.

  • 6th India - Republic of Korea Foreign Policy and Security Dialogue (FPSD) India MEA 13 Feb 2026 -- Shri P. Kumaran, Secretary (East) paid a visit to the Republic of Korea (ROK) on 12-13 February 2026, to co-chair the 6th Foreign Policy and Security Dialogue (FPSD) with his ROK counterpart Mr. Park Yoon-joo, First Vice Foreign Minister, Ministry of Foreign Affairs of ROK, on 13th February 2026.

  • Security Council Press Statement on Terrorist Attack in Islamabad, Pakistan UN Security Council 13 Feb 2026

  • Virtual Trade Ministerial Meeting on Critical Minerals hosted by Ambassador Jamieson Greer, United States Trade Representative Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 13 Feb 2026

  • Bangladesh Nationalist Party secures victory in general election Press TV 13 Feb 2026 -- The Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) has secured a victory in the general election, which was held 18 months after prolonged political unrest ousted the country's former prime minister, Sheikh Hasina.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on the Results of Bangladesh's General Election PRC MofA 13 Feb 2026
  • FCDO statement on parliamentary elections in Bangladesh UK FCDO 13 Feb 2026 -- FCDO statement on parliamentary elections in Bangladesh
  • Voting ends in Bangladesh's national parliament election People's Daily 13 Feb 2026 -- The voting in Bangladesh's national parliament election ended at 4:30 p.m. local time on Thursday.

  • Concerns over Cuba's deepening economic crisis OHCHR 13 Feb 2026 -- "Given the dependence of health, food, and water systems on imported fossil fuels, the current oil scarcity has put the availability of essential services at risk nationwide."
  • UN sounds alarm over US-generated energy crisis in Cuba Press TV 13 Feb 2026 -- The United Nations has sounded the alarm over the energy crisis in Cuba after it was cut off by the US President Donald Trump administration from fuel supplied by Venezuela.

  • Statement of DFA Spokesperson for Maritime Affairs Philippines DFA 13 Feb 2026

  • No: 33, 13 February 2026, Regarding the Resolutions Adopted by the European Parliament Containing Baseless Allegations Concerning Türkiye Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 13 Feb 2026

  • Remulla, China envoy discuss easing tensions, fostering understanding Philippine News Agency 13 Feb 2026 -- Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla met with Chinese Ambassador Jing Quan Thursday to discuss, among other topics, easing tensions between the Philippines and China.

  • Albares defends a strong Europe and a just peace in Ukraine and Palestine in Munich Spain MFA 13 Feb 2026 -- The Minister for Foreign Affairs, European Union and Cooperation, José Manuel Albares, took part in the Munich Security Conference on Friday, 13 February, to defend the values of democracy and a strong and sovereign Europe.

  • World News in Brief: Deadly strikes in Sudan, health systems in South Sudan near the brink, Guterres calls for unity ahead of Ramadan. UN News 13 Feb 2026 -- A sharp increase in drone attacks across the Kordofan region in the centre of Sudan is endangering civilians and damaging critical infrastructure.
